title: Select and Reject 🚦
description: Filter large collections easily using boolean condition blocks.

slides.title: The Filtering Problem 🚦
slides.content:
Imagine you have an Array representing 1,000 user ages.

You want to beautifully extract a new list containing ONLY the users who are over 18.

You could manually `.each` through it, write an `if` statement... or you could use `.select`.

slides.title: The `.select` Method ✅
slides.content:
The Enumerable module provides `.select`. This method loops through your array exactly like `.each`.

However, it tests the block for true/false. If the block returns `true`, it instantly pulls that element out and saves it to a brand new array!

If the block returns `false`, it safely totally ignores it.

slides.title: Selecting Data
slides.content: Run this! Notice how `.select` filters out any numbers that magically match the `num.even?` condition.
slides.codeSnippet:
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]

# Safely extracts only the True results into a new array
evens = numbers.select do |num|
  num.even?
end

puts "Original: #{numbers}"
puts "Evens Only: #{evens}"

quizData.question: What exactly does the powerful `.select` method do to an Array?

options.text: It produces a brand new array containing strictly only the elements that passed the true/false condition.

quizData.explanation: Select is the ultimate filtering tool. If the block says TRUE, the item stays.

slides.title: The `.reject` Method 🚫
slides.content:
What if you want the exact opposite?

You predictably want to silently delete only the users who ARE completely under 18.

You use the spectacularly magical `.reject` method!

slides.title: Rejecting Data
slides.content: Run this! Look how beautifully `.reject` rejects any elements returning perfectly exclusively TRUE to the block.
slides.codeSnippet:
words = ["apple", "banana", "cat", "dog", "elephant"]

# We completely reject any word that starts with 'c'
no_c = words.reject do |word|
  word.start_with?("c")
end

puts no_c

quizData.question: What does the `.reject` method do?

options.text: It creates a new array excluding any items where the block evaluates to true.
It throws an error if false.

quizData.explanation: Reject does the exact opposite of select. It safely creates a brand new array filled exclusively with any objects that threw FALSE.
